name: Button
description: Dense inline toolbar action — 32px (ref.space.400) capsule, label + optional leading / trailing glyph. Visual chrome delegated verbatim to Filter chip — every visual property (min-height, padding, radius, label rung, icon size, container/border/label colors, state overlays, focus ring) mirrors the chip. The semantic divergence is intent: Toolbar Button fires an action; Filter chip toggles a selection; both render with the same DOM and the same CSS classes (`chorus-chip chorus-chip--filter`).

trailingIcon.description: Directional / destination glyph after the label (chevron-down, ×).

default.label: sys.color.onSurface
default.note: The canonical Toolbar Button chrome (mirrors Filter chip's unselected state). The chip-shape signals an inline action that lives within a row of peers (a toolbar, a card footer, a Page bar where the affordance is one of several actions the user might pick).

accent.label: sys.color.onPrimary
accent.note: Brand-blue commit chip — reach for it when the Toolbar Button IS the surface's commit affordance (a Page bar's 'Save', a sheet's 'Confirm', a flow step's 'Done'). The accent fill claims commit-rank attention while keeping the 32px Toolbar Button footprint, so the bar reads at one density even though the action is the screen's most important. Pair sparingly — never two accent Toolbar Buttons in the same row.

inverse.label: sys.color.inverseOnSurface
inverse.note: Inverse-toned chip for use inside an inverse host (snackbars, coach-mark surfaces, dark coach overlays). Same chrome geometry as `default`; the colour pair flips to the inverse cluster so the chip reads against the host's `inverseSurface` fill without a per-host tweak.

focusIndicator.description: Keyboard-focus visual — an accessibility indicator, not a lifecycle state. Composes over whichever lifecycle state the button is in. Visual ring is delegated to Filter chip's composition; this block restates the contract for external readers.
focusIndicator.compositionReason: Action affordance with breathing room around it; the 3px outward extent is reserved by the surrounding toolbar row.
focusIndicator.trigger: :focus-visible (keyboard / programmatic focus, never plain mouse click)

forbidden: Toolbar button used as a primary commit — toolbar is for dense secondary actions in a row
Mixing toolbar buttons with non-toolbar variants in the same row — the row geometry is the contract
